meta.residencyRequirement: Students must complete at least 25% of required competency units (30 of 120 CUs) through WGU residency. Transfer credit and prior learning combined cannot exceed 75% of the degree.
meta.onTimeProgress: Undergraduate full-time students must enroll in a minimum of 12 competency units (CUs) per term (6 months). A competency unit equals one semester credit. This app's default acceleration target (see Settings) is higher than this minimum.
meta.dataNote: SOURCE: WGU Program Guidebook, Bachelor of Arts, Elementary Education, Catalog Version 202603, Published 12/11/2025 (official document provided by the user). This is the verified, official 'Standard Path' course list and competency-unit total (120 CUs across 37 courses) — no course was invented or guessed. IMPORTANT: WGU's official Program Guide lists course names and competency units only; it does not publish course codes (like 'D270') or a definitive OA/PA (Objective vs. Performance Assessment) designation for every course. Course codes shown in MyWGU are assigned at enrollment — check your personalized Degree Plan for exact codes. Where the assessment type is not explicitly stated in the Program Guide, it is labeled 'Verify' rather than guessed. Estimated hours/difficulty are general planning estimates (not published by WGU) except where the guide explicitly states an hour range, which is noted.

courses.name: The Professional Educator
resources: SOE Professional Dispositions and Ethics materials
courses.notes: Prepares WGU students for the profession of educator, covering professional dispositions/ethics and program navigation. The Program Guide states explicitly: 'This is not a transferable course.'

courses.name: Learners and Learning Science
courses.notes: Covers cognitive development, learning theories, neuroscience in education, and developmental milestones from early childhood through adolescence. No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Personalized Learning for Inclusive Classrooms
courses.notes: Personalized learning, IDEA/Section 504, and multi-tiered systems of support (MTSS). No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Creating Positive Learning Environments
courses.notes: Classroom climate, norms/routines, positive behavior supports, trauma-informed and restorative practices. No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Planning Instructional Strategies for Meaningful Learning
courses.notes: Standards-aligned lesson planning, differentiated instruction, and high-leverage instructional practices. No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Assessing and Monitoring Student Learning
courses.notes: Formative/summative assessment design, data analysis, and feedback. No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Instructional Technology and Online Pedagogy
courses.notes: Integrating technology into teaching, online pedagogy, and digital literacy. No Sophia transfer option on the official pathway agreement.

courses.name: Composition: Writing with a Strategy
courses.notes: Official Program Guide estimate: 30–40 hours with no prior knowledge. Covers purpose/context/audience, writing strategies, and editing/revising.

courses.name: Introduction to Communication: Connecting with Others
courses.notes: Communication in professional settings, across contexts and diverse populations.

courses.name: Composition: Successful Self-Expression
courses.notes: Official Program Guide: 'You will demonstrate competency through a performance assessment... 30-40 hours... no prerequisite... no specific technical knowledge needed.'

courses.name: Integrated Physical Sciences
courses.notes: Overview of physics, chemistry, and earth sciences with an emphasis on scientific reasoning.

courses.name: Introduction to Systems Thinking and Applications
courses.notes: NO TRANSFER PATH CONFIRMED. The official Sophia Transfer Pathway Agreement lists this requirement with a blank course-transferred field — no Sophia course currently satisfies it. Official Program Guide estimate: 30-40 hours. Description notes general education math is 'preferred' beforehand. Plan to complete this one at WGU unless your enrollment counselor confirms an alternative.

courses.name: Quantitative Literacy
courses.notes: Algebraic models/functions, geometry, and financial decision-making applications.

courses.name: Technology and Ethics: Emerging Trends and Society
courses.notes: NO TRANSFER PATH CONFIRMED. The official Sophia Transfer Pathway Agreement lists this requirement with a blank course-transferred field — no Sophia course currently satisfies it. Plan to complete this one at WGU unless your enrollment counselor confirms an alternative.

courses.name: Introduction to Biology
courses.notes: Foundational biological sciences: cells, heredity, organism classification, and inter-dependencies with environment.

courses.name: Statistical Data Literacy
courses.notes: Statistics, research, data, and data representation for decision-making; descriptive statistics and graphical depictions.

courses.name: American Politics and the US Constitution
courses.notes: Evolution of representative government, civil rights/liberties, and federal government structure.

courses.name: Natural Science Lab
courses.notes: Official Program Guide: students design and carry out an experiment gathering quantitative data, plus a research-based conclusion — a hands-on/written deliverable, not a proctored exam.

courses.name: Elementary Literacy Curriculum
courses.notes: Language acquisition and literacy concepts: phonological awareness, phonics, word analysis, fluency, comprehension.

courses.name: Early Literacy Methods
courses.notes: Science of reading for grades PK-3: phonemic awareness, language acquisition, emergent reading/writing, MTSS Tier 1, dyslexia supports.

courses.name: Elementary Literacy Methods
courses.notes: Literacy teaching strategies for grades 4-6: vocabulary development, reading comprehension, MTSS Tier 1, dyslexia supports.

courses.name: Foundations of Literacy Through Literature
courses.notes: Using varied, age-appropriate literature to develop reading, writing, speaking, and listening abilities.

courses.name: Literacy Assessment and Interventions
courses.notes: Identifying/addressing literacy challenges (incl. dyslexia) through Tier 1-3 MTSS; diagnostic assessment and targeted interventions.

courses.name: Elementary Mathematics Curriculum
courses.notes: Numbers, operations, algebraic thinking, geometry, measurement, data, statistics, and probability for elementary learners.

courses.name: Early Mathematics Methods and Interventions
courses.notes: Math instruction for PK through early elementary: number sense, basic operations, manipulatives, targeted interventions.

courses.name: Elementary Mathematics Methods and Interventions
courses.notes: Math methods focused on grades 3-6: number operations, geometry, measurement, data analysis, differentiation.

courses.name: Elementary Science Curriculum
courses.notes: Inquiry-based science curriculum: earth, life, and physical sciences; cross-cutting concepts and disciplinary core ideas.

courses.name: Elementary Science and Engineering Methods
courses.notes: Hands-on science/engineering practices, three-dimensional learning approach, scientific literacy.

courses.name: Elementary Social Studies Curriculum
courses.notes: Multidisciplinary: history, geography, civics, economics, culture; standards-aligned curriculum planning.

courses.name: Elementary Social Studies Methods
courses.notes: Effective/innovative methods for teaching social studies; themes, concepts, and modes of inquiry.

courses.name: Elementary Health and Physical Education Methods
courses.notes: Fundamental movement skills, health/wellness habits, and inclusive health/PE instruction.

courses.name: Elementary Fine Arts Methods
courses.notes: Dance, drama, music, and visual arts instruction integrated with other content areas.

courses.name: Elementary Disciplinary Literacy
courses.notes: Reading/writing/speaking/listening demands specific to content disciplines; specialized language structures and text features.

courses.name: Early Clinical in Elementary Education
courses.notes: Immersive real-classroom observation under an experienced teacher's mentorship, with reflective practice. Start placement paperwork early.

courses.name: Advanced Clinical in Elementary Education
courses.notes: Official Program Guide: includes 'a pedagogical performance assessment.' Taken immediately prior to Student Teaching I as part of an intensive clinical experience.

courses.name: Student Teaching I in Elementary Education
courses.notes: First part of the mandatory two-part student teaching series. Full-time supervised classroom placement plus required synchronous sessions. Must align with the host school's academic calendar.

courses.name: Student Teaching II in Elementary Education
courses.notes: Final part of the two-part series. Culminates in a final evaluation determining eligibility for licensure recommendation.
